repairs.evidence: Two of three isolated blind reviewers classified every horizontal A/B pair as screen-right/screen-right; the hard 090-vs-270 cardinal pair failed.
repairs.root_condition: The generated second half-loop retained row 9's screen-right head yaw and did not adopt the approved 270 screen-left cardinal family.
repairs.next_action: Regenerate the complete coherent row 10 with the fourth approved cardinal pose as a hard screen-left anchor for 202.5 through 337.5.
repairs.properties_to_preserve: Kiko identity, neutral green palette, full physical eye construction, shared scale, planted feet, baseline, vertical direction progression, and clean pose separation.

repairs.evidence: The screen-left head directions passed labeled review, but the torso rotated into profile and the tail flipped from screen-left to screen-right at 157.5->180 and 337.5->000.
repairs.root_condition: The generator changed the whole body orientation to achieve leftward gaze instead of keeping Kiko's planted lower body anchored.
repairs.next_action: Regenerate the complete row with a front-facing torso, both feet planted, tail locked low on screen-left, and direction expressed through full eyes, head, snout, and neck.
repairs.properties_to_preserve: Correct screen-left head yaw, Kiko identity, green palette, physical eyes, vertical direction progression, scale, baseline, and clean separation.

result.evidence: All seven left-half poses project the nose, eye aim, and head screen-left; the front belly, both feet, lower body, and tail remain registered with the tail low on screen-left in every cell; both row-boundary joins are continuous.
